mentions.note: IPO priced at $135 but at risk of catastrophic blowout if syndicate desks lose control; retail mania and unguided market orders could spike it to 4–5 trillion valuation unsustainably. Monitor opening behavior closely; do not chase if it gaps violently higher.
IPO priced at $1.75T; syndicate desk control is critical. Uncontrolled retail demand and index inclusion could spark unsustainable spike to $4-5T valuation, then correction—avoid chasing.

IPO reportedly 4x oversubscribed but Cramer wants to see 10x to be confident. Key risk: speculator cohort will flip shares on day one, creating oversupply and poor opening performance (like Figma, Cerebrus). Best IPO outcome: retail + early-stage institutions (non-sellers); worst: dominated by flippers. Warns allocation holders: be cautious if you get full ask or over-allocation.
Risk of market-order-driven opening spike (valuation $5T) followed by sharp reversal like Cerebrus deal; retail inflows may be too hot; no near-term profitability; IPO execution risk high.

IPO slated for $1.77T valuation at $135/share with 30% retail allocation. Long-term thesis strong (Starlink, orbital data centers) but opening vulnerable to market-order retail buyers who may get crushed if stock opens 2x+ higher.
Mentioned as key Computex client; IPO expected within 2 weeks at potential $1.8T valuation. Complex lockup structure (60% locked until mid-2027) supports stock; Cramer targets $4T possible valuation.

IPO June 12 targeting $2T valuation; financials don't justify it (revenue $18.7B, GAAP losses, horrible free cash flow); Starlink segment attractive (4x subscriber growth, 29% EBITDA growth Q1); Space division weak (revenue down 28% Q1, EBITDA halved); AI segment money pit. Anthropic $1.25B/month deal and Cursor acquisition decision upcoming. Rarely pays to bet against Musk but stock will be expensive; put limit order at $2T market cap equivalent.

Expected to file confidentially, targeting mid-June offering; reported $75B raise at $1.75T valuation (largest IPO ever); concerns: Musk's direct involvement in share allocation, non-standard lockups, retail bag-holding risk, XAI cash burn mixed into narrative.
Elon Musk targeting IPO by July 2026 at $88B valuation; massive supply shock risk ($8B+ in new stock) and potential to cannibalize other mega-IPOs.
$800B valuation (up from $210B in June 2024), potential mega-IPO 2026, but $22–24B projected revenue doesn't clearly justify valuation; wait for IPO clarity.
